definitions.text: To apply a force to (an object) such that it moves away from the person or thing applying the force.
examples: In his anger he pushed me against the wall and threatened me.
You need to push quite hard to get this door open.

definitions.text: To continually attempt to persuade (a person) into a particular course of action.
To press or urge forward; to drive.
examples: to push an objection too far; to push one's luck

definitions.text: A short, directed application of force; an act of pushing.
examples: Give the door a hard push if it sticks.

definitions.text: An act of tensing the muscles of the abdomen in order to expel its contents.
examples: One more push and the baby will be out.
Some details got lost in the push to get the project done.
Let's give one last push on our advertising campaign.
